title: EIP-8149: Multi KZG Point Evaluation Precompile
description: Adds a new precompile that verifies multiple KZG openings against a single EIP-4844 blob commitment in one call. The existing point-evaluation precompile costs 50,000 gas per opening; this batch interface verifies multiple openings together with a linear gas model, reducing overhead for contracts that need to read several blob values such as fraud proofs.
author: Chris Mata (@protocolwhisper)
laymanDescription: Currently, reading multiple values from a blob on-chain requires paying a high gas fee for each value separately. This proposal lets contracts verify many blob values at once in a single cheaper call, making fraud proofs and other blob-reading operations significantly less expensive.

endUsers.description: No direct impact. Users interacting with rollups or blob-dependent applications may benefit from lower fees if contracts adopt batch verification.

appDevs.description: Contracts reading multiple blob values (e.g., fraud proofs) can call one precompile instead of many, reducing gas costs and simplifying logic.

walletDevs.description: No impact. The new precompile does not change transaction formats, signing flows, or wallet-facing interfaces.

toolingInfra.description: Tooling that traces or simulates precompile calls must add support for the new multi-point evaluation address and its input/output format.

layer2s.description: Rollups using blob data for fraud proofs or data availability verification benefit directly from cheaper batch KZG verification on L1.

stakersNodes.description: No direct impact on stakers or node operators beyond the standard upgrade to support the new precompile.

elClients.description: Must implement the new precompile including multi-point KZG verification with BLS12-381 pairings, input validation, and gas metering.

clClients.description: No consensus-layer changes required. The precompile operates entirely within the execution layer.

benefits: Reduces gas costs for contracts verifying multiple blob values by batching KZG proofs.
Enables more practical on-chain fraud proofs that need to read several blob fields.
Preserves backward compatibility with existing EIP-4844 transactions and the current precompile.
Bounds input size with MAX_MULTI_POINTS to prevent abuse while allowing meaningful savings.
tradeoffs: Adds a new precompile that all execution clients must implement and maintain.
Gas constants are TBD and must be carefully benchmarked to reflect actual computational cost.

scaleBlobs.description: Directly reduces the cost of verifying blob data on-chain, making blob-dependent applications like fraud proofs more viable.
